title: gcp-sm: put() with pinned ?version= violates WritableSecretStore contract
description: Trigger: construct GcpSmBackend from_uri with ?version=1 (or any pinned version), call put(), then call get(). Bad behavior: put() creates a new GCP SM version (e.g. version 2) but get() is hardwired to self.version='1' so returns the old value. This directly violates WritableSecretStore contract: 'A subsequent call to get on the same or a different instance pointing at the same URI must return the written value.' Root cause: put() calls add_version_url() which always adds a new version; get() calls access_url() with the stored version string which never changes. With version='latest' it works because the new version becomes latest; with any numeric pin it silently fails the round-trip.
